item.title: NATO at breaking point: Transatlantic rupture accelerates under US escalation
item.description: The war in Iran and US President Donald Trump’s renewed push to annex Greenland have triggered what may be the deepest fracture in the 77-year history of the NATO alliance. By early April 2026, relations between the US and its European allies appear to have reached a breaking point, with European leaders openly discussing “Plan […]The post NATO at breaking point: Transatlantic rupture accelerates under US escalation appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln spotted 200 km away from Iran
item.description: LONDON,- US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln has entered the Gulf of Oman and is now approximately 200 km to the south of Iran’s coast, BBC reported, citing satellite data. According to BBC, it was the closest approach to Iran by a US vessel since the start of the US attack on Iran. The carrier […]The post US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln spotted 200 km away from Iran appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: VFEX market capitalisation hits US$3.85bn
item.description: Surging demand for United States dollar-denominated assets has pushed the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) to a market capitalisation of US$3.85 billion, driven by sharp price gains, major listings and a continued shift in investor preference away from the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE). According to stockbrokers IH Securities’ March 2026 Monthly Snapshot, VFEX recorded an […]The post VFEX market capitalisation hits US$3.85bn appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: UK-based director in US$22k Mazoe orange crush theft
item.description: A 33-year-old company director has appeared in court facing theft charges after allegedly orchestrating a scheme that prejudiced Schweppes Zimbabwe of more than US$22 000 worth of Mazoe Orange Crush. The accused, Tatenda Masawi, appeared before Harare magistrate Ruth Moyo over the weekend and was not asked to plead. The complainant is Schweppes Zimbabwe, represented […]The post UK-based director in US$22k Mazoe orange crush theft appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zim should seek new tobacco markets to absorb output surge
item.description: Zimbabwe needs to aggressively seek new tobacco export markets to maintain firm domestic prices, as traditional buyers can no longer absorb the country’s high output volumes, the Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Lands, Agriculture, Fisheries, Water and Rural Development has said. The parliamentary committee, chaired by Chivi South legislator Felix Saul Maburutse, has undertaken fact-finding visits […]The post Zim should seek new tobacco markets to absorb output surge appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: World Bank notes signs of stability, potential in Zimbabwe despite regional headwinds
item.description: Zimbabwe has recorded a positive fiscal balance and is showing early signs of macroeconomic stabilisation, according to the World Bank’s Spring 2026 Africa Economic Update, even as much of the region struggles with rising debt and energy shocks.Zimbabwe trade fair The report, Making Industrial Policy Work in Africa, highlights that Zimbabwe is one of a […]The post World Bank notes signs of stability, potential in Zimbabwe despite regional headwinds appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: US$500m coal-to-fertiliser power move to end imports
item.description: The Government is facilitating new investments in the country’s fertiliser value chain, with plans afoot to inject US$500 million into coal-to-fertiliser production to bolster domestic supply and insulate the country against external shocks. According to the Ministry of Industry and Commerce, representatives from Chinese firms Sunny Yi Feng and Wintrue Holdings recently met with the […]The post US$500m coal-to-fertiliser power move to end imports appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: ‘Tight liquidity to anchor ZiG stability beyond 2026’
item.description: THE ZiG exchange rate is likely to remain stable beyond 2026, supported by tight liquidity conditions and restrained money supply growth, thinktank Africa Economic Development Strategies says . Speaking at a dialogue on geopolitical risks posed by the US-Israeli war against Iran, AEDS executive director Professor Gift Mugano said tight ZiG liquidity has curtailed speculative […]The post ‘Tight liquidity to anchor ZiG stability beyond 2026’ appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Charamba Defends Constitutional Amendment, Dismisses Criticism as Disinformation
item.description: HARARE – Presidential spokesperson George Charamba has mounted a vigorous defence of the proposed Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill, dismissing criticism from opposition groups and sections of civil society as “gratuitous disinformation” and insisting the reform process is both lawful and routine. Speaking in a wide-ranging interview, Charamba—who serves as Deputy Chief Secretary for Presidential […]The post Charamba Defends Constitutional Amendment, Dismisses Criticism as Disinformation appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe Recalls Azithromycin Batch Over Quality Defect, Raising Effectiveness Concerns
item.description: Harare – Zimbabwe’s medicines regulator has ordered the urgent recall of a widely used antibiotic after quality concerns were identified during routine testing, raising alarms over its effectiveness and implications for patient care. The Medicines Control Authority of Zimbabwe (MCAZ) confirmed that Azithromycin 500mg USP Tablets, Batch Number 24050191, are being withdrawn from circulation after […]The post Zimbabwe Recalls Azithromycin Batch Over Quality Defect, Raising Effectiveness Concerns appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Offset makes shocking entry to stage after scary casino shooting
item.description: Offset is already back on stage just days after being shot in Florida. The rapper made his return over the weekend during a performance at the University of Arkansas’s Rowfest on April 11, where he was initially wheeled onto the stage before rising to his feet to cheers from the crowd. Videos from the night show […]The post Offset makes shocking entry to stage after scary casino shooting appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: 4,305 inmates in Zimbabwe walk free under Presidential amnesty
item.description: A wave of joy swept through correctional facilities across Zimbabwe on Monday as thousands of inmates walked to freedom under a Presidential Amnesty.A total of 4,305 prisoners are set to benefit from ...
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News
